title: भगवद्गीता अध्याय २ - साङ्ख्ययोग
devanagariText:
॥ अथ द्वितीयोऽध्यायः - साङ्ख्ययोगः ॥

सञ्जय उवाच —
तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम्।
विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः॥१॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम्।
अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन॥२॥

क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते।
क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप॥३॥

अर्जुन उवाच —
कथं भीष्ममहं सङ्ख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन।
इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन॥४॥

गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्
श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके।
हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव
भुञ्जीय भोगान्रुधिरप्रदिग्धान्॥५॥

न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो
यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः।
यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषामस्
तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः॥६॥

कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः
पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसम्मूढचेताः।
यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्चितं ब्रूहि तन्मे
शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम्॥७॥

न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद्
यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम्।
अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धं
राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम्॥८॥

सञ्जय उवाच —
एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तपः।
न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह॥९॥

तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः॥१०॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे।
गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः॥११॥

न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः।
न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम्॥१२॥

देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा।
तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति॥१३॥

मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः।
आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत॥१४॥

यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ।
समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते॥१५॥

नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः।
उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः॥१६॥

अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।
विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति॥१७॥

अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः।
अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत॥१८॥

य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्।
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते॥१९॥

न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्
नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥२०॥

वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम्।
कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम्॥२१॥

वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि।
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा-
न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही॥२२॥

नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः॥२३॥

अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च।
नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः॥२४॥

अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते।
तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि॥२५॥

अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम्।
तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि॥२६॥

जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च।
तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि॥२७॥

अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत।
अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना॥२८॥

आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेनम्
आश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः।
आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः शृणोति
श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित्॥२९॥

देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत।
तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि॥३०॥

स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि।
धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते॥३१॥

यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम्।
सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम्॥३२॥

अथ चेत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं सङ्ग्रामं न करिष्यसि।
ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि॥३३॥

अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम्।
सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते॥३४॥

भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः।
येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम्॥३५॥

कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥४७॥

योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते॥४८॥

दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय।
बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः॥४९॥

बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते।
तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम्॥५०॥

यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति।
तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च॥५२॥

श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला।
समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि॥५३॥

अर्जुन उवाच —
स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव।
स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम्॥५४॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान्।
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते॥५५॥

दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः।
वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते॥५६॥

यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम्।
नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता॥५७॥

यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता॥५८॥

इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते।
तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि॥६७॥

या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी।
यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः॥६९॥

आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं
समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्।
तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे
स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी॥७०॥

विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः।
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति॥७१॥

एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति।
स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति॥७२॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भगवद्गीतासूपनिषत्सु ब्रह्मविद्यायां योगशास्त्रे
श्रीकृष्णार्जुनसंवादे साङ्ख्ययोगो नाम द्वितीयोऽध्यायः ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Dvitiyo'dhyayah - Sankhya Yogah ||

Sanjaya Uvacha —
Tam Tatha Kripaya-Avishtam-Ashru-Purna-Akulekshanam.
Vishidantam-Idam Vakyam-Uvacha Madhusudanah.||1||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Kutas-Tva Kashmalam-Idam Vishame Samupasthitam.
Anarya-Jushtam-Asvargyam-Akirti-Karam-Arjuna.||2||

Klaibyam Ma Sma Gamah Partha Naitat-Tvayy-Upapadyate.
Kshudram Hridaya-Daurbalyam Tyaktvo-Ttishtha Parantapa.||3||

Arjuna Uvacha —
Katham Bhishmam-Aham Sankhye Dronam Cha Madhusudana.
Ishubhih Pratiyotsyami Puja-Arhav-Arisudana.||4||

Gurun-Ahatva Hi Maha-Anubhavan
Shreyo Bhoktum Bhaiksham-Api-Iha Loke.
Hatva-Artha-Kamams-Tu Gurun-Ihaiva
Bhunjiya Bhogan Rudhira-Pradigdhan.||5||

Na Chaitad-Vidmah Kataran-No Gariyo
Yad-Va Jayema Yadi Va No Jayeyuh.
Yan-Eva Hatva Na Jijivishamah
Te'vasthitah Pramukhe Dhartarashtrah.||6||

Karpanya-Dosho-Pahata-Svabhavah
Prichchhami Tvam Dharma-Sammudha-Chetah.
Yach-Chhreyah Syan-Nishchitam Bruhi Tan-Me
Shishyas-Te'ham Shadhi Mam Tvam Prapannam.||7||

Na Hi Prapashyami Mama-Apanudyad
Yach-Chhokam-Uchchhoshanam-Indriyanam.
Avapya Bhumav-Asapatnam-Riddham
Rajyam Suranam-Api Cha-Adhipatyam.||8||

Sanjaya Uvacha —
Evam-Uktva Hrishikesham Gudakeshah Parantapah.
Na Yotsya Iti Govindam-Uktva Tushnim Babhuva Ha.||9||

Tam-Uvacha Hrishikeshah Prahasann-Iva Bharata.
Senayor-Ubhayor-Madhye Vishidantam-Idam Vachah.||10||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Ashochyan-Anvashochas-Tvam Prajna-Vadamsh-Cha Bhashase.
Gata-Asun-Agata-Asumsh-Cha Na-Anushochanti Panditah.||11||

Na Tv-Eva-Aham Jatu Na-Asam Na Tvam Neme Jana-Adhipah.
Na Chaiva Na Bhavishyamah Sarve Vayam-Atah Param.||12||

Dehino'smin Yatha Dehe Kaumaram Yauvanam Jara.
Tatha Deha-Antara-Praptir-Dhiras-Tatra Na Muhyati.||13||

Matra-Sparshas-Tu Kaunteya Shito-Ushna-Sukha-Duhkha-Dah.
Agama-Apayino'nityas-Tams-Titikshasva Bharata.||14||

Yam Hi Na Vyathayanty-Ete Purusham Purusharshabhah.
Sama-Duhkha-Sukham Dhiram So'mritatvaya Kalpate.||15||

Na-Asato Vidyate Bhavo Na-Abhavo Vidyate Satah.
Ubhayor-Api Drishto'ntas-Tv-Anayos-Tattva-Darshibhih.||16||

Avinashi Tu Tad Viddhi Yena Sarvam-Idam Tatam.
Vinasham-Avyayasya-Asya Na Kashchit-Kartum-Arhati.||17||

Antavanta Ime Deha Nityasyo-Uktah Sharirinah.
Anashino'prameyasya Tasmad-Yudhyasva Bharata.||18||

Ya Enam Vetti Hantaram Yash-Chainam Manyate Hatam.
Ubhau Tau Na Vijanito Nayam Hanti Na Hanyate.||19||

Na Jayate Mriyate Va Kadachin
Nayam Bhutva Bhavita Va Na Bhuyah.
Ajo Nityah Shashvato'yam Purano
Na Hanyate Hanyamane Sharire.||20||

Veda-Avinashinam Nityam Ya Enam-Ajam-Avyayam.
Katham Sa Purushah Partha Kam Ghatayati Hanti Kam.||21||

Vasamsi Jirnani Yatha Vihaya
Navani Grihnati Naro'parani.
Tatha Sharirani Vihaya Jirna-
Ny-Anyani Samyati Navani Dehi.||22||

Nainam Chhindanti Shastrani Nainam Dahati Pavakah.
Na Chainam Kledayanty-Apo Na Shoshayati Marutah.||23||

Achchhedyo'yam-Adahyo'yam-Akledyo'shoshya Eva Cha.
Nityah Sarva-Gatah Sthanur-Achalo'yam Sanatanah.||24||

Avyakto'yam-Achintyo'yam-Avikaryo'yam-Uchyate.
Tasmad-Evam Viditva-Ainam Na-Anushochitum-Arhasi.||25||

Atha Chainam Nitya-Jatam Nityam Va Manyase Mritam.
Tatha-Api Tvam Maha-Baho Naivam Shochitum-Arhasi.||26||

Jatasya Hi Dhruvo Mrityur-Dhruvam Janma Mritasya Cha.
Tasmad-Apariharye'rthe Na Tvam Shochitum-Arhasi.||27||

Avyaktadini Bhutani Vyakta-Madhyani Bharata.
Avyakta-Nidhanany-Eva Tatra Ka Paridevana.||28||

Ashcharya-Vat Pashyati Kashchid-Enam
Ashcharya-Vad Vadati Tathaiva Cha-Anyah.
Ashcharya-Vach-Chainam-Anyah Shrinoti
Shrutva-Apy-Enam Veda Na Chaiva Kashchit.||29||

Dehi Nityam-Avadhyo'yam Dehe Sarvasya Bharata.
Tasmat-Sarvani Bhutani Na Tvam Shochitum-Arhasi.||30||

Sva-Dharmam-Api Cha-Avekshya Na Vikampitum-Arhasi.
Dharmyad-Dhi Yuddhach-Chreyo'nyat Kshatriyasya Na Vidyate.||31||

Yadrichchhaya Cho-Upapannam Svarga-Dvaram-Apavritam.
Sukhinah Kshatriyah Partha Labhante Yuddham-Idrisham.||32||

Karmany-Eva-Adhikaras-Te Ma Phaleshu Kadachana.
Ma Karma-Phala-Hetur-Bhur-Ma Te Sango'stv-Akarmani.||47||

Yoga-Sthah Kuru Karmani Sangam Tyaktva Dhananjaya.
Siddhy-Asiddhyoh Samo Bhutva Samatvam Yoga Uchyate.||48||

Durena Hy-Avaram Karma Buddhi-Yogad-Dhananjaya.
Buddhau Sharanam-Anvichchha Kripanah Phala-Hetavah.||49||

Buddhi-Yukto Jahati-Iha Ubhe Sukrita-Dushkrite.
Tasmad-Yogaya Yujyasva Yogah Karmasu Kaushalam.||50||

Sthita-Prajnasya Ka Bhasha Samadhi-Sthasya Keshava.
Sthita-Dhih Kim Prabhasheta Kim-Asita Vrajeta Kim.||54||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Prajahati Yada Kaman-Sarvan Partha Mano-Gatan.
Atmany-Eva-Atmana Tushtah Sthita-Prajnas-Tado-Uchyate.||55||

Duhkheshv-Anudvigna-Manah Sukheshu Vigata-Sprihah.
Vita-Raga-Bhaya-Krodhah Sthita-Dhir-Munir-Uchyate.||56||

Esha Brahmi Sthitih Partha Nainam Prapya Vimuhyati.
Sthitva-Asyam-Anta-Kale'pi Brahma-Nirvanam-Richchhati.||72||

|| Iti Shrimad-Bhagavad-Gitasu-Upanishatsu Brahma-Vidyayam Yoga-Shastre
Shri-Krishna-Arjuna-Samvade Sankhya-Yogo Nama Dvitiyo'dhyayah ||
hindiMeaning: द्वितीय अध्याय 'साङ्ख्ययोग' भगवद्गीता का सबसे महत्वपूर्ण अध्यायों में से एक है। इसमें श्रीकृष्ण अर्जुन के शोक को दूर करते हुए आत्मा की अमरता का उपदेश देते हैं। वे बताते हैं कि आत्मा अजर, अमर, अविनाशी है — न इसे शस्त्र काट सकते हैं, न अग्नि जला सकती है। जैसे मनुष्य पुराने वस्त्र त्यागकर नए धारण करता है, वैसे ही आत्मा पुराने शरीर को छोड़कर नया शरीर धारण करती है। फिर कृष्ण कर्मयोग का उपदेश देते हैं — 'कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन' — तुम्हें केवल कर्म करने का अधिकार है, फल में कभी नहीं। अध्याय के अंत में 'स्थितप्रज्ञ' (स्थिर बुद्धि वाले ज्ञानी) के लक्षण बताए गए हैं — जो सुख-दुःख में समान रहता है, कामनाओं से मुक्त है, और इन्द्रियों को वश में रखता है।
benefits: Invokes understanding of the immortality of the soul (atman)
Teaches the philosophy of Nishkama Karma (selfless action)
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of death and attachment to the body
Develops equanimity in success and failure
Contains the most essential verse: Karmanye Vadhikaraste
Describes the qualities of a Sthitaprajna (person of steady wisdom)
Traditionally associated with mental peace and clarity in times of confusion
description: The Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2 - Sankhya Yoga, known in Sanskrit as भगवद्गीता अध्याय २ - साङ्ख्ययोग, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Krishna. This sacred composition is sourced from Bhagavad Gita - Mahabharata and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to grant understanding of the immortality of the soul (Atman), describe the qualities of a Sthitaprajna (person of steady wisdom), and contain the most essential verse: Karmanye Vadhikaraste. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami to invoke divine grace. At 72 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 30 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2 - Sankhya Yoga in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2 — Sankhya Yoga?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2 (भगवद्गीता अध्याय २), titled Sankhya Yoga (the Yoga of Knowledge), is the second chapter of the Srimad Bhagavad Gita and is widely regarded as the master-summary of the entire Gita — containing 72 verses. After Arjuna collapses in vishada at the end of Chapter 1, he formally takes Krishna as his guru in 2.7 (shishyas-te'ham shadhi mam tvam prapannam — I am your disciple, surrendered to you, instruct me). Krishna then begins his teaching with the immortality of the Atman (verses 11-30), the duty of the kshatriya to fight (31-38), introduces both the Sankhya path (jnana — verses 11-30) and the Yoga path (karma — verses 39-53), and culminates in the eighteen verses on the sthita-prajna (the person of steady wisdom, verses 54-72) — perhaps the most beloved daily-parayana passage in all the Gita. (Source: Srimad Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2; Mahabharata Bhishma Parva.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous verse Karmanye vadhikaraste in Chapter 2?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 2.47 is the most quoted verse of the entire Bhagavad Gita: Karmanye vadhikaraste ma phaleshu kadachana, ma karma-phala-hetur-bhur ma te sango'stv-akarmani — You have a right only to action, never to its fruits; let not the fruits of action be your motive, nor let your attachment be to inaction. This single verse is the seed and complete formula of Karma Yoga — the path of selfless action that runs through the entire Gita. It teaches: (1) you have authority (adhikara) only over action, never over its results, (2) the fruits should never be the motive (karma-phala-hetuh), (3) but inaction is also not the answer (ma sango'stv-akarmani). Verse 2.48 completes the formula: Yogasthah kuru karmani sangam tyaktva Dhananjaya, siddhy-asiddhyoh samo bhutva samatvam yoga uchyate — Established in yoga perform actions, abandoning attachment, equipoised in success and failure; this evenness is called yoga. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 2.47-2.48.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Chapter 2 teach about the immortality of the Atman?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 2.11-2.30 contain Krishna's foundational teaching on the eternal Atman — the indestructible Self that survives all bodily death. Verse 2.13 introduces the doctrine: Just as in the body the embodied passes through childhood, youth, and old age, similarly does it pass into another body; the wise are not deluded therein. Verse 2.20: Na jayate mriyate va kadachit — Never is It born, nor does It ever die. Verse 2.22: Vasamsi jirnani yatha vihaya navani grihnati naro'parani — As a person casts off worn-out garments and puts on new ones, so the embodied Self casts off worn-out bodies and enters into others that are new. Verse 2.23: Nainam chhindanti shastrani nainam dahati pavakah, na chainam kledayanty-apo na shoshayati marutah — Weapons cleave It not, fire burns It not, water wets It not, wind dries It not. Verse 2.24-25 declares the Atman as eternal (nityah), all-pervading (sarva-gatah), stable (sthanuh), unmoving (achalo'yam), unmanifest (avyaktah), inconceivable (achintyah) and unchangeable (avikaryah). This teaching dissolves Arjuna's grief at the prospect of killing his kinsmen. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 2.11-2.30.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is the Sthitaprajna and what are the 18 verses of his description?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 2.54-2.72 (the famous Sthitaprajna Lakshana — eighteen verses) describe the person of steady wisdom (sthita-prajna). Arjuna asks (2.54): What is the description of the steady-wise one (sthita-prajnasya ka bhasha samadhisthasya Keshava)? How does he speak, sit, walk? Krishna replies: He casts away all desires of the mind and is satisfied in the Self alone (2.55). Untroubled in pain, free from longing in pleasure, free from raga, bhaya and krodha — he is called a muni of steady wisdom (2.56). Verse 2.62-63 — the famous chain of fall: Dhyayato vishayan pumsah sangas-teshupajayate, sangat sanjayate kamah, kamat krodho'bhijayate, krodhad-bhavati sammohah, sammohat smriti-vibhramah, smriti-bhramshad-buddhi-nashah, buddhi-nashat pranashyati — Brooding on objects produces attachment; from attachment desire; from desire anger; from anger delusion; from delusion loss of memory; from loss of memory destruction of intellect; with destruction of intellect one perishes. The eighteen-verse passage (2.55-72) is recited daily by countless seekers as the supreme yogic standard. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 2.54-2.72.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2 called the master-summary of the entire Gita?
seoFaqs.answer: Chapter 2 is called the Gita-saroddhara (essence-summary of the Gita) because it contains in seed-form every major teaching that the remaining sixteen chapters elaborate: (1) the immortality of the Atman (verses 11-30) — the foundation of Chapters 13, 14, 15; (2) sva-dharma and the duty to fight (31-38) — the foundation of Chapters 3, 18; (3) the introduction of Karma Yoga / nishkama karma (39-53) — the foundation of Chapters 3, 4, 5, 6; (4) the description of the sthita-prajna (54-72) — the foundation of the entire Gita's spiritual psychology, repeated in different forms in Chapters 6, 12, 14. Many acharyas (notably Madhusudana Sarasvati in his Gudhartha Dipika) declare that one who has truly grasped Chapter 2 has grasped the entire Gita. The sthita-prajna verses are recited as a daily chapter parayana by sannyasis. (Source: classical Bhagavad Gita commentarial tradition; Madhusudana Sarasvati's Gudhartha Dipika.)

seoFaqs.question: What is svadharma and the duty to fight teaching in Chapter 2?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 2.31-2.38 give Krishna's first major argument from svadharma. Verse 2.31: Svadharmam-api ch-avekshya na vikampitum-arhasi, dharmyad-dhi yuddhach-chhreyo'nyat kshatriyasya na vidyate — Looking upon your own dharma, you should not waver; for there is nothing higher for a kshatriya than a righteous war. Verse 2.32: Such a war coming unsought is the open gate to heaven for fortunate kshatriyas. Verse 2.33: But if you refuse to fight this righteous battle, you incur sin and lose both your duty and your honour. Verse 2.34: People will speak of your eternal disgrace — and for the honoured, dishonour is worse than death. Verse 2.37: Hato va prapsyasi svargam jitva va bhokshyase mahim, tasmad-uttishtha Kaunteya yuddhaya krita-nishchayah — If killed you shall attain heaven; if victorious you shall enjoy the earth; therefore, O Kaunteya, arise resolved for the battle. Verse 2.38 caps with the equanimity formula: Sukha-duhkhe same kritva labha-labhau jayajayau, tato yuddhaya yujyasva — Treating alike pleasure-pain, gain-loss, victory-defeat, then engage in battle; thus you shall not incur sin. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 2.31-2.38.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2 traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Chapter 2 is recited on Day 2 of an 18-day Gita Parayana cycle, but is also one of the four most-recited daily parayana chapters (along with Chapters 12, 15 and 18) because of its master-summary status. Specific occasions include: Wednesday (Budhvar — Krishna's day), every Ekadashi, Gita Jayanti (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi — Mokshada Ekadashi), Janmashtami, the Sthitaprajna eighteen-verse passage (2.55-72) is recited daily by countless sannyasis and meditators, and Karmanye vadhikaraste (2.47) is the most quoted single verse in modern Hindu life — recited as a workplace mantra, before exams, before any new undertaking, and as the daily reminder of the karma-yoga formula. Many Hindus first memorise Chapter 2 verses 11-30 (Atman immortality) and 47-72 (Karma Yoga and Sthitaprajna) before any other Gita passage. (Sources: Gita Mahatmya; classical Vedanta sadhana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Chapter 2 connect to the verse-by-verse Gita pages on this site?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 2 — Sankhya Yoga contains 72 verses, each studied verse-by-verse on this site at /gita/chapter-2/verse-1 through /gita/chapter-2/verse-72. The verse-level pages provide the Devanagari, transliteration, word-by-word meaning, English translation, Hindi anvaya and commentary by classical acharyas (Shankara, Ramanuja, Madhva, Gyaneshwar) for each shloka — particularly important for the foundational shishyas-te'ham verse (2.7), the Atman immortality verses (2.11-30), the seed Karma Yoga verses (2.47-53), and the Sthitaprajna eighteen-verse passage (2.55-72). This stotra page presents the entire chapter as a single text for parayana — the master-summary of the whole Gita and one of the four pillar-chapters of daily Vedanta-sadhana. The chapter pairs naturally with Chapter 18 (which expands its conclusions) and with Chapter 12 (which extends its Bhakti seed). (Source: Stotra and Gita parayana traditions.)
